questions: Can the same shot be pinned to multiple tracks simultaneously? The code structure supports this (composite key of shotId+trackId), but if it never happens in practice, the lookup helpers could be simplified to match by shotId alone.

Are there any code paths that move individual clips within a group independently (not through the group drag or moveClipToRow paths)? For example, an undo/redo system, external integrations, or programmatic clip manipulation? If so, those paths would also need trackId self-healing.

The sync loop in usePinnedGroupSync fires on a debounced timer. If trackId becomes stale during an interaction, should the sync loop's self-healing update be sufficient, or do we need immediate healing at the drag commit site too? Currently rebuildGroupAfterDrag and translateGroupMembers already update trackId at commit time.

success_criteria.criterion: All 26 vulnerable trackId read sites use either resolveGroupTrackId or findGroupForTrack instead of raw group.trackId
resolveGroupTrackId returns the stored trackId without scanning all rows when clips are on the stored track (fast path works)
findGroupForTrack finds a group even when its stored trackId is stale, by resolving actual clip positions
When a stale group is found via resolution, the mutation output includes the corrected trackId (self-healing)
All existing tests in src/tools/video-editor/ pass without modification (except tests that need updating for the new signatures)
New unit tests cover: exact match fast path, stale trackId resolution, orphaned group fallback, multi-track-per-shot disambiguation
TypeScript compilation passes with no new errors
No new files created beyond test additions — helpers live in existing pinned-group-projection.ts
Fast path (trackId is correct) adds negligible overhead — single row lookup + Set membership check
Self-healing is convergent: after any operation touches a stale group, the stored trackId matches reality
resolveGroupTrackId handles edge case where clips are split across multiple rows (returns row with most clips)
Manual smoke test: pin a shot group, move clips to a different track via drag, verify group operations (unpin, switch to video, sync) still work correctly

assumptions: The same shot CAN be pinned to multiple tracks (the composite key structure supports this), so lookups must not collapse to shotId-only matching.
Groups are small (3-10 clips) and timelines have few tracks (3-8 rows), so the O(rows × clips) slow-path scan in resolveGroupTrackId is negligible.

The standard clip drag, moveClipToRow, and translateGroupMembers paths are the only ways clips move between tracks. There is no separate undo/redo system that replays raw row state without updating groups.
rebuildGroupAfterDrag and translateGroupMembers already correctly update trackId at commit time — the staleness comes from edge cases or less common paths, not the primary drag flow.
The self-healing approach (update trackId when resolution reveals staleness) is safe because trackId is purely derived state — correcting it to match reality cannot break invariants.
